Translate this to French: Beware: small-town bars here are not always good places for the out-of-state visitor to hang out.
Translate this to French: For one thing, northern New Mexico has significant problems with drunk driving, and the concentration of intoxicated drivers is high close to small-town bars.
Translate this to French: Unwanted murals or scribble is known as graffiti.
Translate this to French: While it's far from a modern phenomenon, most people probably associate it with youth vandalizing public and private property using spray paint.

Translate this to French: However, nowadays there are established graffiti artists, graffiti events and "legal" walls. Graffiti paintings in this context often resemble artworks rather than illegible tags.
Translate this to French: Boomerang throwing is a popular skill that many tourists want to acquire.
Translate this to French: If you want to learn throwing a boomerang that comes back to your hand, make sure you have a suitable boomerang for returning.
Translate this to French: Most boomerangs available in Australia are in fact non-returning. It is best for beginners to not try throwing in windy
Translate this to French: A Hangi Meal is cooked in a hot pit in the ground.
Translate this to French: When people don't see moose as potentially dangerous, they may approach too closely and put themselves at risk.
Translate this to French: Drink alcoholic drinks with moderation. Alcohol affects everyone differently, and knowing your limit is very important.

Translate this to French: Possible long term health events from excessive drinking can include liver damage and even blindness and death. The potential danger is increased when consuming illegally produced alcohol.
Translate this to French: Illegal spirits can contain various dangerous impurities including methanol, which can cause blindness or death even in small doses.
Translate this to French: Eyeglasses can be cheaper in a foreign country, especially in low-income countries where labour costs are lower.
Translate this to French: Consider getting an eye exam at home, especially if insurance covers it, and bringing the prescription along to be filed elsewhere.
Translate this to French: High-end brand-name frames available in such areas may have two problems; some may be knock-offs, and the real imported ones may be more expensive than at home.
Translate this to French: Coffee is one of the world's most traded commodities, and you can probably find many types in your home region.
Translate this to French: Nevertheless, there are many distinctive ways of drinking coffee around the world that are worth experiencing.
Translate this to French: Canyoning (or: canyoneering) is about going in a bottom of a canyon, which is either dry or full of water.

Translate this to French: Canyoning combines elements from swimming, climbing and jumping--but requires relatively little training or physical shape to get started (compared to rock climbing, scuba diving or alpine skiing, for example).
Translate this to French: Hiking is an outdoor activity which consists of walking in natural environments, often on hiking trails.
Translate this to French: Day hiking involves distances of less than a mile up to longer distances that can be covered in a single day.
Translate this to French: For a day hike along an easy trail little preparations are needed, and any moderately fit person can enjoy them.
Translate this to French: Families with small children may need more preparations, but a day outdoors is easily possible even with babies and pre-school children.
Translate this to French: Internationally, there are nearly 200 running tour organizations. Most of them operate independently.
Translate this to French: The Global Running Tours successor, Go Running Tours networks dozens of sightrunning providers on four continents.
Translate this to French: With roots in Barcelona's Running Tours Barcelona and Copenhagen's Running Copenhagen, it was quickly joined by Running Tours Prague based in Prague and others.
Translate this to French: There are many things you have to take into consideration before and when you travel somewhere.

Translate this to French: When you travel, expect things not to be like they are "back home". Manners, laws, food, traffic, lodging, standards, language and so on will to some extent differ from where you live.
Translate this to French: This is something you always need to keep in mind, to avoid disappointment or perhaps even distaste over local ways to do things.

Translate this to French: Travel agencies have been around since the 19th century. A travel agent is usually a good option for a journey which extends beyond a traveller's previous experience of nature, culture, language or low-income countries.
Translate this to French: Although most agencies are willing to take on most regular bookings, many agents specialise in particular types of travel, budget ranges or destinations.
Translate this to French: It can be better to use an agent who frequently books similar trips to yours.
Translate this to French: Take a look at what trips the agent is promoting, whether on a website or in a shop window.
Translate this to French: If you want to see the world on the cheap, for necessity, lifestyle or challenge, there are some ways to do that.
Translate this to French: Basically, they fall into two categories: Either work while you travel or try and limit your expenses. This article is focused on the latter.
Translate this to French: For those willing to sacrifice comfort, time and predictability to push expenses down close to zero, see minimum budget travel.
Translate this to French: The advice assumes that travellers do not steal, trespass, participate in the illegal market, beg, or otherwise exploit other people for their own gain.
Translate this to French: An immigration checkpoint is usually the first stop when disembarking from a plane, a ship, or another vehicle.
Translate this to French: In some cross-border trains inspections are done on the running train and you should have valid ID with you when boarding one of those trains.
Translate this to French: On night sleeper trains, passports may be collected by the conductor so that you do not have your sleep interrupted.

Translate this to French: Registration is an additional requirement for the visa process. In some countries, you must register your presence and address where you are staying with the local authorities.
Translate this to French: This might require filling out a form with the local police or a visit to the immigration offices.
Translate this to French: In many countries with such a law, local hotels will handle the registration (make sure to ask).
Translate this to French: In other cases, only those staying outside of tourist accommodations need to register.  However, this makes the law much more obscure, so find out beforehand.
Translate this to French: Architecture is concerned with the design and construction of buildings. The architecture of a place is often a tourist attraction in its own right.
Translate this to French: Many buildings are quite beautiful to look at and the view from a tall building or from a cleverly-positioned window can be a beauty to behold.
Translate this to French: Architecture overlaps considerably with other fields including urban planning, civil engineering, decorative arts, interior design and landscape design.
